mechanism_narrative: Consumer spending, as proxied by Retail and Food Services Sales (RSAFS), is a primary driver of corporate revenues. Sustained positive growth in RSAFS indicates a healthy and expanding consumer base, leading to higher earnings expectations and consequently, positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS value is 355655.0 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: If the 3-month annualized growth rate of RSAFS is consistently positive, but the S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of such instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and rapid increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a deteriorating labor market, which erodes consumer confidence and spending power. This leads to reduced corporate profits and, in turn, negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.5 percentage points in 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE is 6.7% as of t-1mo, indicating significant stress
failure_condition: If UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over a 3-month period, but S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 40% of such triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is contained and the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) is kept low, it signifies an accommodative monetary policy environment. This reduces borrowing costs and supports economic activity, fostering a positive outlook for corporate earnings and equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: If both CPIAUCSL is below 220 and FEDFUNDS is below 1.0, but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered periods over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth serves as a key indicator for the health of the manufacturing and industrial sectors. During periods of economic recovery, strong INDPRO growth signals increasing demand, capacity utilization, and business investment, which drives positive equity returns.
failure_condition: If INDPRO shows strong growth during a declared economic recovery, but S&P 500 60-day returns are flat or negative over a 1-year period within that recovery phase.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in housing starts (HOUST) indicates a significant contraction in the construction sector and broader economic activity. This signals reduced investment, consumer confidence, and future demand, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST is 791.0 as of t-2mo, indicating a low level of activity
failure_condition: If HOUST drops by more than 10% month-over-month, but S&P 500 5-day returns are positive in more than 60% of such instances over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a robust and expanding job market. This leads to increased household income and consumer spending, which directly translates into higher corporate revenues and, consequently, positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS shows consistent positive month-over-month growth, but S&P 500 60-day returns are negative over any 3-year rolling window during an expansionary period.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) is cut significantly, it signals aggressive monetary easing by the central bank, typically in response to economic weakness or crisis. This aims to stimulate the economy, boost liquidity, and often leads to positive S&P 500 returns as discount rates fall and risk appetite improves.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS is 0.39 as of t-1mo, indicating very low rates
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS is cut by more than 0.25 percentage points month-over-month, but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of such instances during a crisis or deep recession.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of severe economic weakness, a simultaneous contraction in real GDP (GDPC1) and retail sales (RSAFS) indicates a broad and deep decline in aggregate demand. This severely impacts corporate earnings and outlook, driving significant negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 2-quarter annualized growth < 0% AND RSAFS 3-month average growth < 0%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 is 11712.3 as of t-5mo, RSAFS is 355655.0 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: If both GDPC1 and RSAFS indicate contraction as per the trigger, but S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 30% of such instances during a severe recession.
